raw_response: Hand near ground, arm bent, moderate force, upward vertical, forward horizontal.
thinking:

Next, ARM. The action is throwing, so before the action, the arm would probably be bent. Maybe holding the object, so the arm is bent at the elbow.

HAND position. The object is on the table, so the hand would start at waist height. You reach down to grab it from the table, so near ground would be too low. Waist height makes sense because you bend down to pick it up from the table.

Vertical direction (VD). Throwing down is not typical. Usually, you throw upward to give it momentum. So upward direction.

Horizontal direction (HD). Forward is the primary direction when throwing, so forward.

Putting it all together: Hand near ground (since you bend down to grab from the table), arm bent, moderate force, upward vertical, forward horizontal. So the sentence should combine these elements using the options given.

Then, when you throw, your arm is bent, you move your hand forward and upward with moderate force.
